name: Damon Baker
currentRole: Senior Director, Developer & Publisher Relations, PlayStation
connectionToCompany: Oversees third-party developer relationships for PlayStation platform, engaging with GPU and middleware technology vendors including NVIDIA for PC/cross-platform title optimization
sourceNote: Publicly known Sony Interactive Entertainment executive cited in developer relations contexts and GDC presentations

name: Nick Baker
connectionToCompany: Involved in Xbox hardware platform decisions including GPU architecture evaluation; Microsoft historically evaluated NVIDIA GPUs before transitioning to AMD for Xbox 360 and later consoles
sourceNote: Microsoft Xbox hardware division leadership referenced in industry reporting

name: Heather Lorier
connectionToCompany: Works on Xbox developer tooling and graphics API support relevant to NVIDIA-powered PC titles shipping alongside Xbox versions; DirectX ecosystem overlaps with NVIDIA GPU capabilities
sourceNote: Xbox developer experience leadership referenced in Microsoft developer documentation and GDC sessions

name: Andrew Goossen
connectionToCompany: Led Xbox graphics architecture decisions; Microsoft's original Xbox used NVIDIA GPU (NV2A), making Goossen's predecessor work directly relevant to NVIDIA's historical console GPU business and competitive landscape
sourceNote: Publicly cited in Xbox Series X architecture reveal documentation and Hot Chips presentations

name: Dominic Mallinson
formerRole: VP, Virtual Reality, Sony Interactive Entertainment
connectionToCompany: Led PlayStation VR technology development; PSVR and related rendering pipeline research involves awareness of NVIDIA's GPU and VR rendering innovations (foveated rendering, VRWorks) as competitive and collaborative reference points
sourceNote: Publicly known Sony Interactive Entertainment R&D executive referenced in VR industry coverage and CES presentations
